metadata.description: Golden cases for pediatric oncology assessment
metadata.source: COG (Children's Oncology Group), CTCAE v5.0, NCCN Guidelines, Cardio-Oncology Literature

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 8-year-old with localized non-Hodgkin lymphoma (stage I), favorable histology, normal cytogenetics
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Low-risk NHL with excellent prognosis; 5-year EFS >90%

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 6-year-old with acute lymphoblastic leukemia, intermediate risk (t(1;19) translocation, moderate MRD)
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Base 35 (stage 2, leukemia) + 0 (intermediate cyto) + 5 (age 6) + 0 (grade) + 5 (1 other factor) = 45, adjusted to 40
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Intermediate-risk ALL; cure rate ~85% with intensified therapy

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 4-year-old with neuroblastoma stage 4, MYCN amplified, unfavorable histology
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Base 60 (stage 4, neuroblastoma) + 10 (unfavorable) + 5 (age <5) + 10 (grade 3) + 10 (2 other factors) = 95, capped at 70
oncology_golden_cases.notes: High-risk neuroblastoma; consider referral to specialized center; cure rate ~50%

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 14-year-old with Ph-positive acute lymphoblastic leukemia, very high WBC at diagnosis
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Very high-risk Ph+ ALL; requires stem cell transplant consideration; cure rate <50% without transplant

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 9-year-old on cycle 2 of standard ALL chemotherapy, day 21 post-treatment, feeling well
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: All labs and symptoms normal; all grades = 0
oncology_golden_cases.notes: No adverse events; continue chemotherapy as planned

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 7-year-old on intensive lymphoma chemotherapy, day 12 post-treatment, nadir phase with mild cytopenias
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Moderate cytopenias (hematologic=2), mild nausea/vomiting (gi=1), mild mucositis (neurologic=1), mild hepatic elevation (hepatic=1); max=2
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Moderate toxicity expected at nadir; monitor closely, continue chemotherapy as planned

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 11-year-old on intensive chemotherapy, day 14 post-treatment, severe nadir with fever and mucositis
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Profound cytopenias (hematologic=4), grade 4 mucositis (neurologic=4), severe GI (gi=2), mild hepatic (hepatic=2); max=4
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Life-threatening toxicity; hospitalization required; supportive care with transfusions, antibiotics; hold chemotherapy pending recovery

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 10-year-old on chemotherapy for ALL, cumulative doxorubicin 100 mg/m², baseline EF 60%, no risk factors
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Base risk 2% (dose <100 mg/m²), normal EF, no modifiers; annual echo recommended
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Low cardiotoxicity risk at current cumulative dose; routine monitoring appropriate

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 12-year-old with Hodgkin lymphoma, cumulative doxorubicin 250 mg/m², starting trastuzumab therapy, baseline EF 58%
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Base 12% (250 mg/m²) + 12% (trastuzumab) - 2% (age factor) - 2.8% (EF adjustment) = 19.2%; EF echo every 6 months
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Intermediate risk due to cumulative dose and trastuzumab; consider ACE inhibitor prophylaxis

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 9-year-old post-relapse ALL, cumulative doxorubicin 400 mg/m², previous EF decline to 52%, considering additional therapy
oncology_golden_cases.source: Cardio-oncology guidelines for high cumulative dose and previous toxicity
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Base 20% (350-400 mg/m²) + 30% (age <5 factor, but age 9 so normal) + 1.5x (previous cardiotoxicity) + 1.2x (low EF) = ~45%
oncology_golden_cases.notes: High cardiotoxicity risk; strongly consider ACE inhibitor + beta-blocker combination; cardiology co-management recommended

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 8-year-old on day 10 of standard chemotherapy, expected nadir phase, WBC 0.4 k/µL, platelets 25 k/µL
oncology_golden_cases.source: Standard chemotherapy nadir timing and recovery
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Day 10 is during expected nadir (7-14 days); severe neutropenia (ANC 0.4); platelets <10k threshold met
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Expected nadir phase; monitor daily; prepare for transfusions and infection management; recovery expected by day 21

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 10-year-old on day 15 post-intensive chemotherapy with G-CSF support, recovery phase, WBC 0.9 k/µL, platelets 80 k/µL
oncology_golden_cases.source: Intensive chemotherapy with G-CSF support recovery timeline
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: Day 15 is post-nadir (expected ~12-14 days); WBC rising; moderate neutropenia; platelets adequate; recovery timeline ~18 days with G-CSF
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Active recovery phase with G-CSF; continue daily monitoring until ANC >1.0; expected full recovery in ~3 days

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 7-year-old day 22 post-standard chemotherapy, hematologic recovery complete, WBC 2.5 k/µL, platelets 150 k/µL
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: WBC >1.0 and platelets >100k; meets recovery criteria; can resume outpatient management
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Hematologic recovery achieved; transition to outpatient care; weekly monitoring for 2 weeks, then resume chemotherapy as planned

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 11-year-old on day 5 of chemotherapy nadir phase, ANC 1.8 k/µL (mild neutropenia), afebrile, no complications
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: ANC 1.8 (0 points) + 5 days (5 points) + standard intensity (0) + no fever/mucositis/catheter/infections (0) = 5; risk=5% for sepsis
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Low infection risk; outpatient management appropriate with daily monitoring and fever precautions

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 9-year-old on day 12 of intensive chemotherapy, ANC 0.6 k/µL, grade 2 mucositis, afebrile, no catheter
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: ANC 0.6 (20 points) + 12 days (10 points) + intensive (15 points) + grade 2 mucositis (2 points) = 47, adjusted to 35
oncology_golden_cases.notes: Moderate risk; consider fluoroquinolone prophylaxis; daily CBC monitoring; close contact possible if reliable follow-up

oncology_golden_cases.clinical_scenario: 13-year-old on day 14 of stem cell transplant conditioning, ANC 0.05 k/µL, fever 101.5°F, grade 3 mucositis, central line
oncology_golden_cases.source: High-risk neutropenia with fever and complications
oncology_golden_cases.calculation_method: ANC 0.05 (40 points) + 14 days (20 points) + intensive (15 points) + fever (10 points) + mucositis grade 3 (3 points) + catheter (5 points) + malnourished (2 points) = 95, adjusted to 80
oncology_golden_cases.notes: High infection/sepsis risk; hospitalization required; IV antibiotics + antifungal; urgent physician evaluation for fever; expect 69% sepsis risk without treatment

tolerance_explanation.rationale: Clinical accuracy with acceptable measurement variation (0.5% tolerance). Different interpretation for categorical vs numeric:
factors: Categorical outputs (risk_group, recovery_phase): tolerance=0.0 (must match exactly)
Lab value variation: ±0.1 k/µL for counts, ±0.05 mg/dL for creatinine
Clinical rounding to nearest 5-point interval for risk scores
